content:
7 min read
Just now

--

The 2025–26 data tells a story nobody’s putting in their listicles: AI didn’t replace engineers — it created a verification economy. Here’s how to get paid in it.
Three numbers came out of the last twelve months of engineering research, and together they explain the entire AI-and-jobs debate better than any hot take you’ve read.
84% of developers now use or plan to use AI coding tools, per the latest Stack Overflow survey data.
29% trust the output — down from 40% in 2024.
72% of organizations have already suffered at least one production incident caused by AI-written code, according to Harness’s State of AI in Software Engineering report, a survey of 900 engineers and technical leaders across the US, UK, France, and Germany.
Read those again. Adoption went up while trust went down — at the same time. That’s not how failed technologies behave; failed technologies get abandoned. And it’s not how mature technologies behave either; mature technologies earn trust. This is something different: an industry that has decided AI-generated code is too valuable to skip and too unreliable to ship unchecked.
That gap — between what AI produces and what production demands — is where every valuable engineering career of the next decade…
